pattern: The maximum achievable minimum power is a threshold you can binary search for directly, because checking 'can every city reach at least X power' is a single greedy left-to-right sweep that always spends new stations as far right as their range allows.
hints: Ask a yes/no question instead of the original one: 'can every city be guaranteed at least X power using at most k new stations?' If that question can be answered quickly for any X, binary searching over X finds the true maximum.

To answer the yes/no question, sweep cities left to right tracking each city's current power (its original stations plus any new ones already placed within reach); whenever a city falls short of X, new stations must be added right now -- and there is no reason not to place them at the rightmost city still in range, since that helps the most future cities.

Track how many extra stations affect each upcoming position with a running delta rather than rescanning the whole window on every addition, and fail the feasibility check the instant total stations committed exceeds k.

edge_inputs.description: small mixed array with a modest range and a few new stations to place
every city starts at zero stations, all power must come from new ones
range of zero, so no city shares power with a neighbor
range wide enough that every city sees the entire array
no new stations may be built at all
a single city, so every new station must land in the same place
the weakest cities sit between two strong ones, tempting a solution to place new stations directly on the weak spot instead of at the window's right edge
two separate low points tied for the minimum, so a single placement must be shown to help at most one of them at a time

stress.description: large random station counts with a random range and a large station budget
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 100000))
    stations = [rng.randint(0, 100000) for _ in range(n)]
    r = rng.randint(0, n - 1)
    k = rng.randint(0, 1000000000)
    return [stations, r, k]
